Farn Pollucel PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
November 7,2003
Thomas A. Willis
Remcho, Johansen & Purcell
201 Dolores Avenue
San Leandro, CA94577
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. .4.-03-198
Dear Mr. Willis:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Lynne McGhee
:r^gTli?+e post-governmental employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
'Act').' Please note that nothing in this letter should be conskued to evalu ate any
conduct that may have already taken place. Our advice is based on the facts presented in
your request; the Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission') does not act as a
finder of fact when it provides advice. (rn re oglesby (1975) I Fppc ops. 71.)
QUESTTON
As a former employee of the California Public Utility Comrnission (..CpUC,), is
Ms. McGhee barred by the one-year ban, under the Act's post-employrnent provisions,
from appearing on behalf of her current employer, the Califomiu Wuto Service Company
("Cal Wate,f), in rate proceedings conducted before administrative law judges at the
CPUC?
CONCLUSION
For purposes of the one-year ban, the Act provides that an appearance by a former
state administrative agency ernployee before his or her fonner state administrative agency
does not include an appearance before an administrative law judge. Thus, Ms. McGhee
is not prohibited by the one-year ban from representing her current employer in rate
proceedings before the CPUC's adminiskative law judges.
I Govemment Code sectiorx 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
Re:
===


File No. A-03-198
Page No. 2
FACTS
Ms. McGhee is currently employed as associ ate corporate counsel for Cal Water,
an investor-owned utility regulated by the CPUC. Prior to working at CalWater, Ms.
McGhee was employed atthe CPUC, where she worked as a staff attorneyin that
agency's legal division, and also as alegal advisor to two successive cpuc
commissioners. Her employment with the CPUC ceased on June 6,2003. While at the
CPUC, Ms. McGhee did not work on anymatters involving Cal Water; Cal Water
proceedings were handled by the commissioners and their water advisor, not Ms.
McGhee. Ms. McGhee did work on some matters involving other waterutilities, but most
of her work was in the unrelated areas of energy, transportation and telecommunications.
Ms. McGhee anticipates that prior to the one-year anniversary of her separation
from state service she will be called upon, in her capacity as Cal Waier,s associate
general counsel, to represent Cal Water in rateproceedings before the CpUC,s
administrative law judges. Rate proceedings of this nature are "on-the-record,,
proceedings in which the hearing is recorded, parties arepresent, and evidentiary
inquiries are made. The presiding administrative.law judge is present at all times during
these proceedings.
AI\[ALYSIS
Public officials who leave state service are subject to two tlpes ofpost-
governmental ernployment restrictions under the Act. The first is a permanent
prohibition on advising or representing anyperson for compensation in any judicial or
otherproceeding (including contracts) in which the official participated whiie in state
service. (Sections 87401 and 87402.) The second is a one-year ban on making any
appearance or cornmunication for compensation before the official's former agency, or
officer or employee thereof, for the purpose of influencing any administrative, legisiative
or other specified action (including contracts). (Section n+oa; Although yo., *"
requesting advice concerning only the one-year ban, the matters you raisi potentiully
implicate the permanent ban and we will discuss that also.
One Year Ban
The Act prohibits a designated employee, for a period of one year afterleaving
state service, from being paid to communicate with or appear before ihri. fo.mo agency
"for the purpose of influencing administrative or legislative action," or "any action or
proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awardin& or revocation of a permit,
license, gffit, or contract, or the sale or purchase of goods or property-,, (Sedtion
87406(d)(l).) A "former agency'' includes any state administrative agency the designated
employee formerly worked for or represented during the l2-month period before he or
she left state service and also includes any agency, commission, departrnent or division
whose budget, personnel and other operations are controlled by the former agency.
(Rogulation 1 8746. I (bX6).)


File No. ,4.-03-198
PageNo. 3
"An appearance or communication includes, but is not limited to, conversing by
telephone or in person, coresponding with in writing or by eleckonic transmission,- :
attending a meeting, and delivering or sending any communication." (Regulation
18746.2.) By statute, the reference in the Act to "appearance" does not includ e "al
appeaxaflce in a court of law, before an administrative law judge, or before the'Workers,
Compensation Appeals Board." (Section 87406(d)(l); Graves Advice Letter, No. I-98-
rul@).) Therefore, the one-year ban would not prohibit Ms. McGhee from appearing in
an administrative hearing before an administrative law judge in order to represent Cal
Water in rate proceedings.'
Permanent Ban
Sections 87401 and87402 (collectively, the "permanentban')prohibit a former
state administrative official from advising or representin g arry person, other than the State
of Californi a, for compensation in any judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding in
which the official participated while in state senrice. Specifically, section B74Ol
provides:
'1Io former state administrative official, after the termination of his or
her employment or term of officg shall for compensation act as agent or
attomey for, or otherwise represent, any other person (other than the State
of california) before any court or state administrative agerrcy or any
officer or employee thereof by making any formal or informal appearance,
or by making any oral or written communication with the intent to
influence, in connection with anyjudicial, quasi-judicial or other
proceeding if both of the following apply:
(a) The state of california is a party or has a direct and substantial
interest.
(b) Theproceeding is one in which the formerstate administrative
o ffi cial participated. "
In addition, under section 87402 a former state administrative official shall not,
for compensation, "aid, advise, counsel, consult or assist in representing any otherperson
(except the State of California) in anyproceeding in which the official would be
prohibited from appearing under Section 8740L " This is a lifetime ban and applies to
any proceeding in which Ms. McGhee participated while a state administrative official at
the CPUC. It includes proceedings in which she participated, but left state employ before
the proceedings concluded. (Costa Advice Letter, No. 4-98-003.)
The permanent ban, however, does not prohibit Ms. McGhee from representing
her employer in any new proceeding before the CPUC, even though her employer may
' This exception, however, has been interpreted to apply only to hearings, preliminary hearings,
settlement negotiations and otler formal matters before a judge in which all parties are present and a
franscripl recording or other record of the former official's contact with his or her former agency is made.
(Anderson Advice Letter, No. A-98-159; Weil advice Letter, No. A-97-247.) Your facts indicatl that these
criteria are met with respect to these rate proceedings.


FileNo. 4-03-198
Page No. 4
have been a party to a prior proceeding in which she participated while in state employ.
A proceeding involving the same parties, but different factual or legal issues from itrose
considered in a prior proceeding, or a proceeding involving different parties, are both
deerned to be 'hew proceedings" for purposes of the permanent ban. -(Grimm Adice
Letter, No. A-99-086.)
Although you describe generally that Ms. McGhee did not advise the CpUC
commissioners with respect to any matters involving Cal Water, you have not requested
our advice on applying the permanent ban to her situation. Thus, we make no attempt to
identiff anyproceedings in which she maybe subject to the permanent ban and, apirt
from calling this matter to your attention, offerno advice on how the permanent ban
might apply to her.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
.Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By:
Counsel, Legal Division
KLGjg
